_instructions: Add a state by copying an object into the 'states' array. Fields: name, abbr, capital, emoji (used as the big 'image'), color (hex), facts (array of 3+ short kid-friendly strings).

states.name: Alabama
facts: The rockets that went to the Moon were built in Alabama!
Alabama has beautiful white sand beaches by the Gulf of Mexico.
The state flower is the Camellia — a pretty pink flower.

states.name: Alaska
facts: Alaska is the BIGGEST state in all of America — it's huge!
Alaska has giant brown bears, bald eagles, and moose.
In summer, the sun shines even at midnight — it never gets dark!

states.name: Arizona
facts: The Grand Canyon is in Arizona — one of the biggest holes on Earth!
Saguaro cactus plants in Arizona can live for 200 years!
Arizona is very sunny and warm — it barely ever snows there.

states.name: Arkansas
facts: You can dig for real diamonds in Arkansas — and keep them if you find one!
Arkansas has beautiful rivers, forests, and hot springs.
Arkansas is called the Natural State for all its beautiful nature.

states.name: California
facts: California is home to Disneyland and Hollywood movies!
The biggest trees in the world — Giant Sequoias — grow in California.
California grows more fruits and vegetables than any other state.

states.name: Colorado
facts: Colorado has the Rocky Mountains — great for skiing in winter!
Denver is called the Mile High City because it sits so high up.
Colorado has bighorn sheep — they climb super steep rocky mountains!

states.name: Connecticut
facts: Connecticut was one of the very first states in America!
The first hamburger was sold in Connecticut way back in 1895.
Connecticut has beautiful harbors with sailboats.

states.name: Delaware
facts: Delaware was the FIRST state to join the United States — number 1!
Delaware is called the First State because of this.
Delaware is one of the tiniest states, but it came first!

states.name: Florida
facts: Florida is home to Disney World — the most magical place on Earth!
Florida has real alligators living in its swamps and lakes.
Florida is called the Sunshine State — it's warm and sunny almost every day!

states.name: Georgia
facts: Georgia grows more peaches than almost any other state — yum!
Georgia is called the Peach State.
Atlanta, the capital, hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics!

states.name: Hawaii
facts: Hawaii is the only state made of islands in the middle of the ocean!
Hawaii has volcanoes — some are still active and making new land!
Hawaii grows pineapples and macadamia nuts.

states.name: Idaho
facts: Idaho grows more potatoes than any other state!
Idaho is called the Gem State because it has so many beautiful gemstones.
The Snake River runs through Idaho making beautiful canyons.

states.name: Illinois
facts: Chicago, in Illinois, is famous for its yummy deep-dish pizza!
President Abraham Lincoln lived in Illinois most of his life.
Illinois is called the Land of Lincoln.

states.name: Indiana
facts: Indiana is famous for the Indianapolis 500 — a super fast car race!
Indiana is called the Hoosier State.
Indiana has beautiful sand dunes along Lake Michigan.

states.name: Iowa
facts: Iowa grows more corn than almost any other state!
Iowa is called the Hawkeye State.
Iowa has rolling green hills and farms as far as you can see.

states.name: Kansas
facts: Kansas is covered in golden sunflowers — millions of them!
Kansas is famous for big flat prairies that stretch forever.
Dorothy from The Wizard of Oz was from Kansas!

states.name: Kentucky
facts: Kentucky is famous for beautiful horses and the Kentucky Derby horse race!
Kentucky has the longest cave system in the world — Mammoth Cave!
Kentucky is called the Bluegrass State because of its special blue-green grass.

states.name: Louisiana
facts: Louisiana has the most fun party in America called Mardi Gras!
New Orleans has the most amazing jazz music in the world!
Louisiana is famous for yummy food like gumbo and beignets.

states.name: Maine
facts: Maine is famous for its yummy lobsters caught from the cold ocean!
Maine is the most northeastern state — it sees the sunrise first!
Maine has beautiful rocky coastlines with tall lighthouses.

states.name: Maryland
facts: Maryland is famous for delicious blue crabs from Chesapeake Bay!
Maryland is called the Old Line State.
The US Naval Academy is in Annapolis, Maryland — where navy heroes train.

states.name: Massachusetts
facts: The Pilgrims landed near Plymouth, Massachusetts way back in 1620!
The very first Thanksgiving was celebrated in Massachusetts.
Boston has lots of exciting history from America's very beginning.

states.name: Michigan
facts: Michigan is surrounded by the Great Lakes — the biggest freshwater lakes ever!
Most cars made in America come from Detroit, Michigan.
Michigan is shaped like a mitten — you can use your hand as a map!

states.name: Minnesota
facts: Minnesota has over 10,000 lakes — more lakes than almost anywhere!
Minnesota gets very cold and snowy in winter.
Minnesota is called the Land of 10,000 Lakes.

states.name: Mississippi
facts: The mighty Mississippi River runs along the edge of this state!
Mississippi is famous for blues music — a type of music that tells stories.
Mississippi is called the Magnolia State for its beautiful magnolia trees.

states.name: Missouri
facts: The Gateway Arch in St. Louis is the tallest arch in the whole world!
Missouri is called the Show-Me State.
The Missouri River, one of America's longest rivers, flows through here.

states.name: Montana
facts: Montana is called Big Sky Country because the sky looks SO wide and huge!
Glacier National Park in Montana has amazing icy mountains.
Montana has more elk, deer, and bears than people!

states.name: Nebraska
facts: Nebraska has Chimney Rock — a tall rock that looks just like a chimney!
Nebraska has flat wide prairies in the middle of America.
Nebraska is called the Cornhusker State because it grows lots of corn.

states.name: Nevada
facts: Las Vegas, Nevada has the most bright lights and shows in the world!
Nevada is mostly desert — hot, dry, and very sunny.
Nevada has mysterious Area 51 — a famous secret place!

states.name: New Hampshire
facts: New Hampshire was one of the very first states to want independence!
New Hampshire has the most beautiful colorful leaves in autumn.
New Hampshire's motto is 'Live Free or Die!' — very brave words!

states.name: New Jersey
facts: New Jersey has famous beaches and fun boardwalks to play on!
Thomas Edison — who invented the light bulb — lived in New Jersey.
New Jersey is called the Garden State because it grows lots of vegetables.

states.name: New Mexico
facts: New Mexico has beautiful red and orange desert landscapes!
New Mexico has the most hot air balloon festivals in the whole world!
Ancient people built their homes right into the cliffs there long ago.

states.name: New York
facts: The Statue of Liberty is in New York — a huge gift from France!
New York City is the biggest city in America — millions of people live there!
Niagara Falls, one of the world's biggest waterfalls, is in New York.

states.name: North Carolina
facts: The Wright Brothers flew the very FIRST airplane in North Carolina in 1903!
North Carolina has beautiful mountains AND beaches.
North Carolina grows more sweet potatoes than any other state!

states.name: North Dakota
facts: North Dakota has the beautiful Badlands — strange bumpy rock hills.
North Dakota grows lots of sunflowers and wheat.
North Dakota is very cold in winter but gorgeous in summer!

states.name: Ohio
facts: More astronauts came from Ohio than any other state — including Neil Armstrong!
Ohio is called the Buckeye State for its buckeye trees.
The Rock and Roll Hall of Fame is in Cleveland, Ohio — a music museum!

states.name: Oklahoma
facts: Oklahoma is in Tornado Alley — big spinning tornadoes sometimes visit!
Oklahoma has amazing Native American history and colorful powwows.
Oklahoma is called the Sooner State.

states.name: Oregon
facts: Oregon has Crater Lake — the deepest and most blue lake in all of America!
Oregon's forests are SO green because it rains a lot there.
Oregon has both beaches AND mountains — you can visit both in one day!

states.name: Pennsylvania
facts: The Liberty Bell is in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania — it rang when America became free!
America's Declaration of Independence was signed in Pennsylvania!
Pennsylvania has Hershey — the chocolate capital of the US!

states.name: Rhode Island
facts: Rhode Island is the SMALLEST state in America — tiny but mighty!
You can drive across Rhode Island in under one hour!
Rhode Island has beautiful harbors filled with sailing boats.

states.name: South Carolina
facts: South Carolina has beautiful beaches with warm water and soft sand!
South Carolina grows lots of peaches and sweet tea is very popular there.
The palmetto tree is South Carolina's symbol — it's on the state flag!

states.name: South Dakota
facts: Mount Rushmore is in South Dakota — 4 giant president faces carved in rock!
South Dakota has the Badlands — a landscape that looks like the moon!
South Dakota has lots of wild bison roaming free on the prairies.

states.name: Tennessee
facts: Nashville, Tennessee is the music capital of America — especially country music!
Tennessee is called the Volunteer State.
Great Smoky Mountains National Park is in Tennessee — very beautiful!

states.name: Texas
facts: Texas is the second BIGGEST state in all of America!
Texas was once its own country — it's called the Lone Star State!
Cowboys and ranches are famous in Texas — yeehaw!

states.name: Utah
facts: Utah has Arches National Park with over 2,000 natural stone arches!
The Great Salt Lake is SO salty you float in it like a cork!
Utah has five national parks — more than almost any other state!

states.name: Vermont
facts: Vermont makes more maple syrup than any other state — yum on pancakes!
Vermont has beautiful snowy winters and colorful fall leaves.
Vermont is one of the smallest and most peaceful states.

states.name: Virginia
facts: More US presidents were born in Virginia than any other state — 8 presidents!
Virginia is called the Old Dominion — one of the first 13 states.
Jamestown, Virginia was the first English settlement in America!

states.name: Washington
facts: Washington grows more apples than any other state!
Seattle has a famous Space Needle tower you can see for miles.
Mount Rainier in Washington is a giant snow-covered sleeping volcano!

states.name: West Virginia
facts: West Virginia has the most beautiful mountains in the eastern USA!
West Virginia is called the Mountain State.
West Virginia separated from Virginia during the Civil War to stay united.

states.name: Wisconsin
facts: Wisconsin makes more cheese than any other state — it's the Dairy State!
Wisconsin also produces lots of milk and butter.
Wisconsin has beautiful green forests and sparkling blue lakes.

states.name: Wyoming
facts: Yellowstone National Park is in Wyoming — it has amazing geysers!
Old Faithful is a geyser that shoots hot water into the air every hour!
Wyoming has the most wild bison roaming free in all of America!
